# Migrate GridThumbDragDevIndex whitebox-e2e onto the locked-column fixture (split scroll-telemetry vs locked-cell)

## Context

`test/playwright/e2e/GridThumbDragDevIndex.spec.mjs` was skipped when the devindex locked-column config was removed, and was deferred from the #12945 / #12936 fixture migration (which re-homed `GridColumnCrossBodyDnD` + `GridColumnOverdragScroll` onto the new `examples/grid/lockedColumns` fixture).

## The Problem

GridThumbDragDevIndex is a **mixed** spec — its two tests have different needs:

- **Horizontal drag / locked-cell stability** — queries `.neo-locked-start` / `.neo-locked-end` cells and asserts they stay visually frozen during horizontal scroll. A locked-column concern that fits the `examples/grid/lockedColumns` fixture.
- **Vertical scroll telemetry / row-pinning** — Profiles 1-7 (wheel + synthetic + thumb drags) asserting zero blank-frames / jitter. A vertical-virtualization concern that needs a **many-row streaming grid**, not a small locked-column fixture; its `beforeEach` also depends on devindex's stream-stop button.

Retargeting cleanly means splitting these two concerns — real per-spec work distinct from standing up the locked-column fixture itself, which is why it was deferred.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Split the locked-cell horizontal-stability test out and re-home it onto `examples/grid/lockedColumns` (un-skip it).
- [ ] Re-home or re-scope the vertical scroll-telemetry / row-pinning test onto a many-row grid that survives the devindex lock removal (devindex post-removal or `examples/grid/bigData`), independent of locked columns.
- [ ] Remove the `ticket-ref-ok` skip annotation from the spec once both land.

## Related

#12936 / #12945 (the fixture this builds on); #12933 / #12935 (devindex lock removal — the forcing event).

- 2026-06-12T09:16:58Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `464940a` - "test(grid): restore real locked-cell horizontal-stability coverage on the fixture (#12949)

GridThumbDragDevIndex's horizontal locked-cell stability assertions went vacuous when the devindex app lost its locked columns: with no .neo-locked-start / .neo-locked-end cells, the checks compared null === null and passed without testing anything.

This re-homes that coverage onto the examples/grid/lockedColumns fixture, where the locked regions are real and the single-column locked-end body now renders its cells. The spec asserts the locked cells are PRESENT before checking stability, so it can no longer pass vacuously.

GridThumbDragDevIndex itself still passes (verified: both its vertical scroll-telemetry and the now-vacuous horizontal test), so this adds genuine coverage rather than fixing a red spec."
- 2026-06-12T09:52:09Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `307d372` - "test(grid): locked-cell stability through optical + large (recycling) horizontal scroll (#12949)

Restores non-vacuous locked-cell coverage on the examples/grid/lockedColumns fixture: under horizontal scroll the centre cells translate optically while locked-start AND locked-end cells stay frozen — through both a small optical scroll and a large scroll that recycles the centre's mounted-column window.

Asserts cell PRESENCE before stability (no vacuous null===null pass — the failure mode that silently lost this coverage on lock-less devindex), plus a worker-oracle recycle signal (the centre's mountedColumns window shifts after the large scroll) so the virtualization leg is evidenced rather than only claimed."
- 2026-06-12T09:59:23Z @tobiu referenced in commit `322de54` - "test(grid): locked-cell stability through optical + large (recycling) horizontal scroll (#12949) (#12968)

Restores non-vacuous locked-cell coverage on the examples/grid/lockedColumns fixture: under horizontal scroll the centre cells translate optically while locked-start AND locked-end cells stay frozen — through both a small optical scroll and a large scroll that recycles the centre's mounted-column window.

Asserts cell PRESENCE before stability (no vacuous null===null pass — the failure mode that silently lost this coverage on lock-less devindex), plus a worker-oracle recycle signal (the centre's mountedColumns window shifts after the large scroll) so the virtualization leg is evidenced rather than only claimed.
